blocks.name: MSSQL-Ops infrastructure (migration foundation, RoboticoOps/reset, diagnostics, docs)

chunks.name: Migration foundation (Ebene A) + conventions SSoT + lint harness
chunks.context_notes: FOUNDATION GATE for the whole plan: db-migrations/README.md is the conventions contract (folder semantics up/functions/views/sprocs/permissions; forbidden USE, GO;, multi-object anytime files, hardcoded JTL IDs; naming Schema.Objekt.sql vs NNNN_snake_case.sql; D10 EKL-boundary rules verbatim) that C2/C3 must obey. lint-migrations.ps1 is the executable form of that contract (§7) — build it here so downstream chunks self-check against it (§7 'Lint läuft je Chunk-Self-Check ab Verfügbarkeit'). §7 is authored last in the plan but pulled into this chunk because it is the test stack / foundation harness. Port from WorkflowProcedures/*: strip 'USE eazybusiness', 'GO;'->'GO', prefer CREATE OR ALTER, keep _SetActionDisplayName registration in the same file as its action proc, add '-- Ported from ...' provenance. Object inventory to port lives in research/5 §3 — produce the mapping table in the chunk report. HARD CONSTRAINTS: edits only inside the worktree worktrees/feature/mssql-ops-infrastruktur; NO writes against any SQL server (read-only /opt/mssql-tools*/bin/sqlcmd -S vm-sql-test1.zdbikes.local -E -C only); no real secrets/keys in files (use {{...}} placeholders). deploy.ps1 stays thin; PROD path needs interactive Y/N confirmation (research/1.1 lesson 5); validate deploy.ps1 with a PowerShell parse check only, never a live run.

chunks.name: RoboticoOps DB + global chain (Ebene B) + reset SP/agent-job orchestration
chunks.context_notes: §2 and §3 are MERGED into one chunk because they are not file-disjoint (§2 declares db-migrations/global/sprocs/reset.StartTestmandantReset/GetResetStatus/ProcessNextResetRequest.sql; §3 fills those exact files plus the reset.internal_* helpers) and because §3 builds directly on the ops.Mandant/ops.Config/ops.ResetRequest tables and signing chain defined in §2. Depends on C1 for the conventions README + lint harness — all files must pass lint-migrations.ps1. Signing model (D6, research/3 Sommarskog): cert RoboticoOpsSigning private key in RoboticoOps, public-only into master via certencoded() (no disk roundtrip); StartTestmandantReset runs WITH EXECUTE AS 'jobstartuser' and is signed; 900_resign_procedures.sql runs everytime to re-add signatures CREATE OR ALTER strips. Job owner sa, no signing in the job. Port reset.internal_* procs from Projekte/Testsystem/* preserving behaviour; SQLCMD vars -> sp_executesql params (payload strings ONLY parameterized, DB/object names ONLY via QUOTENAME — no concatenation of user data into elevated dynamic SQL). Guard every internal proc against @TargetDb='eazybusiness' (triple defense). D9 worker neutralization is NEW (pf_user lock + guarded queue purge; do NOT touch Worker.tTarget — O1). HARD CONSTRAINTS: worktree-only edits; NO writes against any SQL server (read-only sqlcmd only); no secrets in files ({{CertPassword}} token + {{ShopLicense}} placeholders, real values via runbook).

chunks.name: Read-only probes (§4) + hygiene/cleanup scripts (§6) + their runbooks
chunks.context_notes: §4 (read-only probe scripts + validation runbook) and §6 (hygiene analysis scripts + runbook) are merged: both are read-only diagnostic SQL + runbook material, informationally independent of C2's implementation, and file-disjoint from all other chunks — so this runs in PARALLEL with C2. Depends only on C1 (probes live under db-migrations/tests/ and follow the same conventions; the validation runbook can be written from the plan's reset flow without C2's code being finished). All §4 probe SQL is STRICTLY read-only (SELECT/catalog); the implementing agent MAY run them read-only against vm-sql-test1 via /opt/mssql-tools*/bin/sqlcmd -E -C and record results (answering O1/O2/O4 where possible), but 02_worker_discovery is a manual instruction for Lukas (needs a running worker). §6 cleanup scripts (dana sysadmin, tm2 refresh, premig DB) carry a prominent 'manual execution only, production impact' header and every mutating statement is COMMENTED OUT (D13, lint enforces no uncommented writes). HARD CONSTRAINTS: worktree-only edits; NO writes against any SQL server (read-only sqlcmd allowed); no secrets in files.

chunks.name: Architecture doc, 3 plan-scoped ADRs, rollout runbook, deprecation banners, indexes
chunks.context_notes: Documentation chunk, authored LAST so it reflects the as-built code: depends on C1+C2 (ADR adr-module-signing-reset covers D5/D6/D7/D8 = the signing/reset security model built in C2; the architecture doc is a post-implementation snapshot) and on C3 (docs/runbooks/README.md is the index listing C3's testmandant-reset-validierung + hygiene-findings runbooks, and rollout-mssql-ops references the §4 validation runbook). All three ADRs use plan-scoped form: no number, header '# ADR-NNNN: ...' placeholder, Status 'Proposed (plan-scoped — pending promotion)', full knowledge-adr-format sections, bidirectional ## References to this plan; promotion to docs/decisions/NNNN-*.md happens at plan completion (docs/decisions/ does not exist yet — this repo's first ADRs). Load knowledge-adr-format + ~/.claude/templates/adr.md for the ADRs and knowledge-doc-format + ~/.claude/templates/universal.md for the architecture doc/runbooks; all new docs are ENGLISH. NAMING-CONVENTIONS.md is an EDIT (additive only). setup-test-environment.ps1 is an EDIT (top comment banner only, D12 — do not change any functional line). SSoT rule: operating rules live in the architecture doc, not duplicated from the plan. HARD CONSTRAINTS: worktree-only edits; NO writes against any SQL server; no secrets in files.
